data.title: Axalta holder Artisan Partners strongly opposes Akzo deal
data.description:
Axalta Coating (AXTA) holder Artisan Partners said it's against the all-stock merger with Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF).

"As an Axalta shareholder, we believe the only proper response to this proposed transaction is an absolute and resounding `NO,'  Artisan Partners said in a statement on Wednesday.

The statement comes after Axalta (AXTA) on Tuesday announced an all-stock merger with Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) to create a global coatings company valued at approximately $25 billion.

"We are struggling to comprehend what is happening here," Artisan Partners said in the statement. "Axalta and Akzo Nobel are calling this a `merger of equals,' but let’s call it what it really is: selling out of a cheap business that is performing well and taking the currency of a company that has never done well, one that has far inferior assets and financial performance," Artisan Partners said in the statement.

data.title: Aris Mining, Colombia reach settlement to end arbitration; takes full ownership of Soto Norte
data.description:
Aris Mining (ARMN) +4% pre-market Thursday after saying it reached a settlement with Colombia's government to end the arbitration proceeding brought under the Canada-Colombia Free Trade Agreement, ending a dispute dating back to 2018.

Under the settlement, no cash payments were made or contemplated, and the agreement is structured around a series of performance obligations by Aris Mining (ARMN) and multiple Colombian government entities.

Separately, Aris (ARMN) said it agreed to acquire the remaining 49% interest in the Soto Norte joint venture in Colombia from MDC Industry Holding for $80 million, comprised of $60 million in cash and nearly 1.74 million newly issued common shares at $11.50/share.

Aris (ARMN) said full ownership of Soto Norte will enhance its ability to advance the project as a core long-term growth asset, and position it to become a 1 million oz/year gold producer.

data.title: Nasdaq upgraded, MarketAxess downgraded at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley on Thursday upgraded Nasdaq (NDAQ) to Overweight from Equal-Weight on the basis of both cyclical and secular tailwinds, but also downgraded rival MarketAxess Holdings (MKTX) to Equal-Weight from Overweight.

Such tailwinds are expected to drive accelerate revenue growth across Nasdaq's (NDAQ) Solutions businesses into 2026-2027, analyst Michael Cyprys wrote of Nasdaq in a note.

"We see NDAQ as an attractive play on capital markets recovery plus a business transformation story on their shift into higher quality, more recurring revenue streams in large addressable markets," he explained. A revival in dealmaking is set to boost listing fees, "while a supportive macro backdrop for risk assets into 2026 should lift index revenues and support its workflow & insights businesses."

Meanwhile, MarketAxess (MKTX) shares were downgraded due to a potentially benign credit outlook that could weigh on secondary trading volumes into" next year," Cyprys wrote, adding macroeconomic headwinds could limit multiple expansion.

Morgan Stanley's Equal-Weight rate on MKTX compares with the average SA analyst rating and the average Wall Street analyst rating, both at Buy, as well as the SA Quant system rating of Sell.

In premarket trading, NDAQ gained 1.9%, while MKTX slipped 0.2%.

data.title: Cisco to invest in spatial computing firm World Labs
data.description:
Cisco (CSCO) said on Thursday that its venture capital arm, Cisco Investments, will invest an undisclosed amount in spatial computing firm World Labs.

Founded by world renowned computer scientist Dr. Fei-Fei Li, World Labs is driving the next paradigm shift in artificial intelligence, Cisco said in a statement.

The startup, which raised $230M in funding in September 2024, allows computers to understand and interact with 3-D environments and could transform areas such as gaming and robots, Cisco added. The Chuck Robbins-led Cisco said its investment is World Lab's largest strategic investment to date.

“World Labs is driven by a single passion: advancing AI to augment human potential and deliver real-world impact,” said Fei-Fei Li, Co-founder and CEO, World Labs. “Cisco's leadership in secure, scalable infrastructure is a critical enabler for the next generation of AI and the evolution of physical AI. We are thrilled to work with them as we bring our vision to life.”

Dr. Li, who has been referred to as the “godmother of AI,” has extensive experience in the tech industry. She previously served on the board of directors at Twitter (now known as X), as an executive at Google Cloud and various other companies.  

“The evolution of AI from linguistic intelligence to spatial intelligence marks the next revolutionary leap, as models move beyond understanding language to reasoning about, generating, and interacting with the physical world. World Labs is pioneering this frontier with Large World Models,” said Martin Casado, General Partner at venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz. “We've long shared Fei-Fei's vision for spatial intelligence and see huge potential for changing how AI engages with the world. Cisco's investment reflects the importance of secure, scalable infrastructure in advancing this new era of AI.”

Andreessen Horowitz, also known as a16z, is one of the backers of World Labs.

data.title: Georgia-based community banks Morris State Bancshares, Pinnacle Financial to merge
data.description:
Morris State Bancshares (OTCQX:MBLU) and Pinnacle Financial jointly announced on Thursday a strategic combination to form a single community bank in Georgia.

Each outstanding share of Morris common stock will be exchanged for the right to receive 0.1095 shares of the combined company.

Additionally, the company's shareholders will receive a one-time special cash dividend of $0.54 per share prior to the closing of the transaction.

Pinnacle investors are estimated to own ~54% of the outstanding common shares of the combined entity, while Morris shareholders are set to own about 46%.

The corporate headquarters will be in Elberton, Georgia.

Both boards have unanimously approved the definitive agreement.

A new unified name and logo are set to be unveiled before the close of the transaction. The new company's common stock will begin trading on the OTCQX under a new ticker symbol.

The combined company is expected to have $3.8B in assets, $3.3B in deposits, and $2.8B in loans. The entity is estimated to operate 35 branches throughout Georgia.

The new board will consist of nine directors from Pinnacle and eight from Morris.

Pinnacle Chairman and CEO Jackson McConnell will assume the responsibility of board chairman and CEO at the new company. Morris Chairman, President, and CEO Spence Mullis will become the director of community banking.

Pinnacle CFO Scott Wilson will be the CFO, while Morris CFO Chris Bond will take over as chief accounting officer.

The transaction is expected to close at the end of the first quarter or early second quarter of 2026.

data.title: EU demands inclusion in Ukraine peace talks as reports emerge of U.S.-Russia draft plan
data.description:
Senior European diplomats insisted Thursday that neither Ukraine nor the EU can be excluded from any negotiations aimed at ending Russia’s nearly four-year-old invasion, the Associated Press reported Thursday.

The demands came as reports emerged of a U.S.-Russia draft proposal that would require major Ukrainian concessions at a moment when Kyiv is grappling with a corruption scandal and intense battlefield pressure. European defense stocks (EUAD) sold off Wednesday amid media coverage of the draft.

Axios first reported the existence of the proposal, which, according to a person familiar with its contents, would grant Russia several of the demands it has pushed since launching its full-scale assault. The leaked plan added to the political strain on President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, who is simultaneously trying to shore up European military support, manage unrest at home over alleged energy-sector corruption, and hold the line against a larger Russian force.

Arriving at a meeting of EU foreign ministers in Brussels, the bloc’s top diplomat, Kaja Kallas, stressed that “For any plan to work, it needs Ukrainians and Europeans on board.”

Her view was echoed by other ministers. Germany’s Johann Wadephul said “all negotiations about a ceasefire, regarding the further peaceful development of Ukraine, can only be discussed and negotiated with Ukraine. And Europe will have to be included.”

It remained unclear Thursday whether European governments had been shown the document, reportedly crafted by U.S. and Russian envoys. The version circulating in diplomatic circles would require Ukraine to give up territory, something the Zelenskyy government has repeatedly rejected and which is barred under Ukraine’s constitution.

The White House declined to discuss the reported proposal. Deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller said only that Trump wants “to reach a settlement in the Ukraine-Russia war, so that we can have peace in Europe and we can end the killing and the slaughter of so many innocents.”

Despite months of U.S. outreach, including unsuccessful attempts earlier this year to spur negotiations, no diplomatic breakthrough has materialized. A delegation of senior U.S. Army officials, including Army Secretary Dan Driscoll, visited Kyiv on Thursday to evaluate conditions on the ground and assess whether new peace efforts might be feasible, according to U.S. officials.

Territorial concessions at the core

The person familiar with the draft plan said it envisions ceding parts of Ukraine to Russia and restricting certain Western-supplied weapons. It would also roll back some forms of U.S. military assistance. Under the proposal, Moscow would effectively control the entire Donbas region, territory of high strategic and symbolic value, portions of which remain in Ukrainian hands.

The draft was spearheaded by Trump’s special envoy Steve Witkoff and Russian sovereign wealth fund head Kirill Dmitriev, a longtime confidant of Vladimir Putin, the person said.

Any settlement requiring Ukraine to relinquish land would be overwhelmingly unpopular domestically and would violate Ukrainian law. Zelenskyy has firmly rejected such terms in the past.

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on X late Wednesday that Washington “are and will continue to develop a list of potential ideas” for a durable peace, noting that any agreement “will require both sides to agree to difficult but necessary concessions.”

In Moscow, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Thursday that “there are no consultations per se currently underway” with Washington on ending the war, though he acknowledged “there are certainly contacts.”

Danish Foreign Minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen expressed uncertainty about the proposal’s credibility, remarking: “First and foremost, we have to find out whether it is really the big boys who (are) behind this plan or not… we have to really find out what is up and what is down.”

Europe seeks a seat at the table

European officials have grown increasingly worried that Washington may be pursuing a separate track with Russia that sidelines both Kyiv and the EU. Many EU diplomats argue that Putin’s calls for peace are not genuine, pointing to Russia’s continued strikes, including a recent attack on Ternopil that killed 26 and injured 93.

Kallas said that “if Russia really wanted peace, it could have … agreed to (an) unconditional ceasefire already some time ago.”

Because Trump halted direct military aid to Ukraine, Europe has assumed a larger role by financing U.S.-made weapons for Kyiv, giving EU governments considerable influence over any eventual settlement.

Polish Foreign Minister Radosław Sikorski underscored that point, saying: “We commend peace efforts, but Europe is the main supporter of Ukraine and it’s, of course, Europe’s security that’s at stake. So we expect to be consulted.”

data.title: AstraZeneca's Alexion gets FDA approval for Koselugo
data.description:
• Alexion, part of AstraZeneca (AZN), said that the FDA has approved Koselugo for the treatment of adults with neurofibromatosis type 1 who have symptomatic, inoperable plexiform neurofibromas.
  • The  approval by the FDA was based on positive results from KOMET.

data.title: Bear View: Forget Nvidia and payrolls, these bond moves could break the equity bull
data.description:
Investors worried about the sustainability of the bull market should pay attention to Japanese bond yields, according to self-described Uber Bear Albert Edwards.

Edwards, strategist at Societe Generale, posted that while investor focus is on Nvidia's (NVDA) results and delayed U.S. economic data, "the single most important development in markets at the moment is surging long #JGB yields ... The 10 year has broken out and jumped to 1.82% today." (JGBZX)

"Is this what breaks the equity bull market?" (SPY) (QQQ) (DIA)

Edwards said back in May that spiking Japanese bond yields are the most significant risk to global markets since the 2008 crisis-era regime of bond-yield suppression began.

Edwards argued that Japan has historically been an early warning point for major financial turns, adding that Japanese institutions could pull capital home, reversing years of yen-funded carry trades and heavy foreign bond buying, including U.S. Treasuries. (TBT) (TLT) (US10Y) (US30Y)

Edwards warned that U.S. markets are vulnerable because both Treasuries and equities have benefited from persistent Japanese inflows. If higher JGB yields draw domestic investors back, he says the unwind could hit U.S. assets hard and pressure the dollar. (DXY)

data.title: Enphase Energy signs new safe harbor deal projected to bring nearly $68M in revenue
data.description:
• Enphase Energy (ENPH) on Thursday said it signed a new safe-harbor agreement with a major third-party ownership solar financing provider, its third such deal since July 2025.
  • The agreement, covering IQ9 microinverters manufactured in the U.S., is expected to generate nearly $68 million in revenue over 12 to 24 months starting in 2026.
  • Shipments of the microinverters are scheduled to begin in the first quarter of 2026.
  • ENPH +1.85% premarket to $27.39.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sabre subsidiary launches $1B notes offering
data.description:
• Sabre’s (SABR) wholly-owned subsidiary, Sabre Financial Borrower, has announced an offering of $1B aggregate principal amount of senior secured notes.
  • The precise timing, size, and terms of the offering are subject to market conditions and other factors. Sabre Financial will use the gross proceeds from the sale to fund an intercompany loan to Sabre GLBL.
  • SABR +4.3% premarket to $1.6697
  • Source: Press Release
